title: Ogilvy on Advertising
author: David Ogilvy
description: David Ogilvy's definitive guide to the craft, science, and business of advertising. Drawing on decades of agency work, Ogilvy covers what makes a headline pull, when long copy outperforms short, how to use photography and illustration, the principles behind legendary campaigns like Schweppes, Hathaway Shirts, and Rolls-Royce, and when to test ads before launch. Part practitioner's manual, part memoir, the book remains the single most cited reference by working copywriters and marketing directors.
